max.description: Maximum value for the absolute threshold.
max.title: Max

min.description: Minimum value for the absolute threshold.
min.title: Min

sensitivity.description: Sensitivity level for anomaly detection, ranging from 0 to 100.
sensitivity.title: Sensitivity

values_different.description: Count of different cells observed in the sample
values_different.title: Values Different

values_different_estimate.description: Statistical estimate of differences for the full dataset. Only present when sampling was applied. Includes observed value, extrapolated estimate, confidence level, and confidence interval bounds.

histogram_bins.description: Number of histogram bins available (0 if no histogram)
histogram_bins.title: Histogram Bins

top_values_count.description: Number of top enum values available (0 if no enums)
top_values_count.title: Top Values Count

total.description: Total number of rows in exclusive set
total.title: Total

column.description: Column name the rule applies to. When column remapping is enabled, this refers to the dataset-A (canonical) column name — the same name the comparator operates on after remapping. Mutually exclusive with `type`. Exactly one of `column` or `type` must be set.
column.title: Column

equal_if.description: SQL boolean expression OR-ed into the value comparator for the matching column(s). Use {a} and {b} as placeholders for the side-A and side-B column references. Multiple rules matching the same column are all OR-ed together.
equal_if.title: Equal If

type.description: Type-based selector — matches all columns whose Datatype.typename equals this value. The rule is applied to every column of that type after data lands in DuckDB post-fetch. Mutually exclusive with `column`.

column_mapping.description: Map columns with different names between datasets. List of [column_in_A, column_in_B] pairs.
column_mapping.title: Column Mapping

column_rules.description: Extra equality clauses that extend the in-memory diff value comparator. Useful for treating specific value pairs (e.g. legacy sentinels vs NULL) as non-differences. See `ApiColumnRule` for entry shape.
column_rules.title: Column Rules

columns_to_compare.description: Columns to compare between datasets. If set, only these columns are diffed (primary key columns are always included). Column names must match the dataset schema.
columns_to_compare.title: Columns To Compare

compare_duplicates.description: Compare rows with duplicate primary keys. Defaults to true.
compare_duplicates.title: Compare Duplicates

data_source1_id.description: ID of the first data source (Dataset A).
data_source1_id.title: Data Source1 Id

data_source1_session_parameters.description: Snowflake session parameters for Dataset A, e.g. {"QUERY_TAG": "datadiff", "WAREHOUSE": "COMPUTE_WH"}.
data_source1_session_parameters.title: Data Source1 Session Parameters

data_source2_id.description: ID of the second data source (Dataset B). Can be the same as data_source1_id.
data_source2_id.title: Data Source2 Id

data_source2_session_parameters.description: Snowflake session parameters for Dataset B.
data_source2_session_parameters.title: Data Source2 Session Parameters

diff_tolerance.description: Default tolerance for float comparisons. In absolute mode: values within this distance are equal. In relative mode: fraction of difference allowed.
diff_tolerance.title: Diff Tolerance

diff_tolerances_per_column.description: Per-column tolerance overrides. Each entry: {column_name, tolerance_value (>= 0), tolerance_mode: 'absolute'|'relative'}.
diff_tolerances_per_column.title: Diff Tolerances Per Column

exclude_columns.description: Columns to exclude from comparison. Ignored if include_columns is set.
exclude_columns.title: Exclude Columns

file1.description: File URL for Dataset A (s3://, gs://, abfss://, https://). Mutually exclusive with table1 and query1. Requires file1_options.
file1.title: File1

file1_options.description: File format options for file1 (file_type, delimiter, sheet, skip rows).
file1_options.title: File1 Options

file2.description: File URL for Dataset B (s3://, gs://, abfss://, https://). Mutually exclusive with table2 and query2. Requires file2_options.
file2.title: File2

file2_options.description: File format options for file2 (file_type, delimiter, sheet, skip rows).
file2_options.title: File2 Options

filter1.description: SQL WHERE clause for Dataset A (omit the WHERE keyword), e.g. 'status = 1'.
filter1.title: Filter1

filter2.description: SQL WHERE clause for Dataset B (omit the WHERE keyword), e.g. 'status = 1'.
filter2.title: Filter2

include_columns.description: Explicit list of columns to compare. If set, only these columns are diffed.
include_columns.title: Include Columns

infer_pk.description: Infer primary key columns automatically before running the diff. When true, pk_columns may be omitted; the inferred PK is stored back on the diff. Supported configurations: in-db diffs (all input types); cross-db diffs with query inputs on both sides (PK is inferred inside DuckDB after fetch). Not supported: cross-db with table inputs, or any file-based diff — use POST /api/v1/data_sources/{id}/guess_pk and pass pk_columns explicitly. For in-db query inputs, materialization is auto-forced.
infer_pk.title: Infer Pk

infer_pk_avoid_names.description: Column names to skip during PK inference. Matched case-insensitively against actual column names (exact match, no glob/regex). Requires infer_pk=true and pk_columns empty; rejected with 422 otherwise.
infer_pk_avoid_names.title: Infer Pk Avoid Names

infer_pk_avoid_types.description: Extra column types to skip during PK inference, on top of the built-in defaults (float, timestamp, array, boolean, binary, unsupported). Use canonical type names (e.g. 'integer', 'text') or 'db:<RAW_TYPE>' to target a raw warehouse type (e.g. 'db:JSONB'). Requires infer_pk=true and pk_columns empty; rejected with 422 otherwise.
infer_pk_avoid_types.title: Infer Pk Avoid Types

materialization_destination_id.description: Data source ID where materialized diff results are stored.
materialization_destination_id.title: Materialization Destination Id

materialize_dataset1.description: Materialize Dataset A before diffing. Improves speed for heavy queries, filtered non-indexed columns, or transformed primary keys.
materialize_dataset1.title: Materialize Dataset1

materialize_dataset2.description: Materialize Dataset B before diffing. Same use cases as materialize_dataset1.
materialize_dataset2.title: Materialize Dataset2

pk_columns.description: Column names that uniquely identify rows, e.g. ['id'] or ['tenant_id', 'order_id']. Must match actual column names in both datasets. Leave empty and set infer_pk=True to auto-detect.
pk_columns.title: Pk Columns

query1.description: SQL query for Dataset A. Mutually exclusive with table1 and file1.
query1.title: Query1

query2.description: SQL query for Dataset B. Mutually exclusive with table2 and file2.
query2.title: Query2

run_profiles.description: Run column profiling on diff results.
run_profiles.title: Run Profiles

sampling_confidence.description: Sampling confidence level, between 0 and 100 exclusive. Common values: 90, 95, 99, 99.5, 99.9. Use with sampling_tolerance.
sampling_confidence.title: Sampling Confidence

sampling_max_rows.description: Maximum number of rows to sample (absolute count). Alternative to tolerance+confidence and sampling_ratio.
sampling_max_rows.title: Sampling Max Rows

sampling_ratio.description: Sample this fraction of rows. Value between 0 and 1 exclusive (e.g. 0.1 = 10% of rows). Alternative to tolerance+confidence.
sampling_ratio.title: Sampling Ratio

sampling_threshold.description: Minimum row count to activate sampling. Sampling is disabled if the largest table has fewer rows than this.
sampling_threshold.title: Sampling Threshold

sampling_tolerance.description: Sampling tolerance: max fraction of rows with PK errors before sampling is disabled. Value between 0 and 1 exclusive (e.g. 0.001 = 0.1%). Use with sampling_confidence.
sampling_tolerance.title: Sampling Tolerance

sort_array_columns.description: Sort array elements before comparison to ignore insertion order differences.
sort_array_columns.title: Sort Array Columns

table1.description: Table path for Dataset A as a list of path components, e.g. ['schema', 'table'] or ['database', 'schema', 'table']. Mutually exclusive with query1 and file1.
table1.title: Table1

table2.description: Table path for Dataset B as a list of path components, e.g. ['schema', 'table'] or ['database', 'schema', 'table']. Mutually exclusive with query2 and file2.
table2.title: Table2

table_modifiers.description: Table-level modifiers. Allowed values: 'case_insensitive_strings' (ignore string case), 'null_equals_empty_string' (treat NULL and empty string as equal when one warehouse uses NULL and another uses '' for missing text).
table_modifiers.title: Table Modifiers

tags.description: Tags for organizing and filtering diffs.
tags.title: Tags

time_aggregate.description: Time aggregation level when using time_column.

time_column.description: Column name used for time-based filtering or aggregation.
time_column.title: Time Column

time_travel_point1.description: Time travel point for Dataset A. Accepts: negative integer offset (e.g. -130), UTC timestamp (e.g. '2024-01-15T00:00:00'), or a time point hash. Only supported by Snowflake and Databricks.
time_travel_point1.title: Time Travel Point1

time_travel_point2.description: Time travel point for Dataset B. Same format as time_travel_point1.
time_travel_point2.title: Time Travel Point2

algorithm.description: Diff algorithm. 'join' for same-database diffs, 'fetch_and_join' for cross-database or file diffs. Auto-selected if omitted: 'join' when both data sources are the same, 'fetch_and_join' otherwise.

column_mapping.description: Map columns with different names between datasets. List of [column_in_A, column_in_B] pairs.
column_mapping.title: Column Mapping

column_rules.title: Column Rules

compare_duplicates.title: Compare Duplicates

data_source2_id.description: ID of the second data source (Dataset B). Can be the same as data_source1_id.

file2_options.title: File2 Options

exclusives.description: Rows with exclusive primary keys detected in dataset A and B. `None` if table is not ready yet or if materialization wasn't requested. If materialization is completed, for a diff inside a single database the field will contain a list with one element. If diff compares tables in different databases, the list may contain one or two entries.
exclusives.title: Exclusives

column.description: Column name the rule applies to. When column remapping is enabled, this refers to the dataset-A (canonical) column name — the same name the comparator operates on after remapping. Mutually exclusive with `type`. Exactly one of `column` or `type` must be set.
column.title: Column

equal_if.description: SQL boolean expression OR-ed into the value comparator for the matching column(s). Use {a} and {b} as placeholders for the side-A and side-B column references. Multiple rules matching the same column are all OR-ed together. Example: "{a} = '1900-01-01' AND {b} IS NULL".
equal_if.title: Equal If

type.description: Type-based selector — matches all columns whose Datatype.typename equals this value. The rule is applied to every column of that type after data lands in DuckDB post-fetch. Mutually exclusive with `column`. Exactly one of `column` or `type` must be set.

column_name.description: Column name to apply tolerance to.
column_name.title: Column Name

default.description: Default tolerance applied to all columns.
default.title: Default

cron.description: The cron expression that defines the schedule.

ApiKeyAuth.description: Use the 'Authorization' header with the format 'Key <api-key>'
ApiKeyAuth.name: Authorization

info.description:
The Datafold API reference is a guide to our available endpoints and authentication methods.
If you're just getting started with Datafold, we recommend first checking out our [documentation](https://docs.datafold.com).

:::info
  To use the Datafold API, you should first create a Datafold API Key,
  which should be stored as a local environment variable named DATAFOLD_API_KEY.
  This can be set in your Datafold Cloud's Settings under the Account page.
:::
info.title: Datafold API

get.description: Daily/monthly LLM spend + effective caps for the caller's org.

schema.title: Response Get Ci Api V1 Ci  Ci Config Id  Runs Get

get.description:
Retrieves all data sources for the organization, annotated with an `accessible` boolean.

Returns active data sources (not deleted, hidden, or draft). Each DS includes `accessible=True`
if the user is admin or the user's groups overlap with the DS's groups.

schema.title: Response List Data Sources Api V1 Data Sources Get
Response Create New Datasource Api V1 Data Sources Post
Response Get Data Source Types Api V1 Data Sources Types Get
Response Get Data Source Api V1 Data Sources  Data Source Id  Get

post.description:
Executes a SQL query against the specified data source and returns the results.

This endpoint allows you to run ad-hoc SQL queries for data exploration, validation, or analysis.
The query is executed using the data source's native query runner with the appropriate credentials.

**Streaming mode**: Use query parameter `?stream=true` or set `X-Stream-Response: true` header.
Streaming is only supported for certain data sources (e.g., Databricks).
When streaming, results are sent incrementally as valid JSON for memory efficiency.

Returns:
- Query results as rows with column metadata (name, type, description)
- Limited to a reasonable number of rows for performance

parameters.description: Stream results as JSON
parameters.name: stream

schema.description: Stream results as JSON
schema.title: Stream

post.summary: Execute a SQL query against a data source

get.description:
All fields support multiple items, using just comma delimiter
Date fields also support ranges using the following syntax:

- ``<DATETIME`` = before DATETIME
- ``>DATETIME`` = after DATETIME
- ``DATETIME`` = between DATETIME and DATETIME + 1 MINUTE
- ``DATE`` = start of that DATE until DATE + 1 DAY
- ``DATETIME1<<DATETIME2`` = between DATETIME1 and DATETIME2
- ``DATE1<<DATE2`` = between DATE1 and DATE2

post.description:
Launches a new data diff to compare two datasets (tables or queries).

A data diff identifies differences between two datasets by comparing:
- Row-level changes (added, removed, modified rows)
- Schema differences
- Column-level statistics

The diff runs asynchronously. Use the returned diff ID to poll for status and retrieve results.

Cancels a data diff that is currently queued or running.

This operation stops the diff execution and marks it as cancelled. If the diff has already
completed or been cancelled, this operation has no effect and returns the current status.

Use this to stop long-running diffs that are no longer needed or were started with incorrect parameters.

get.description:
Retrieves a comprehensive, human-readable summary of a completed data diff.

This endpoint provides the most useful information for understanding diff results:
- Overall status and result (success/failure)
- Human-readable feedback explaining the differences found
- Key statistics (row counts, differences, match rates)
- Configuration details (tables compared, primary keys used)
- Error messages if the diff failed

Use this after a diff completes to get actionable insights. For diffs still running,
check status with get_datadiff first.
get.summary: Get a human-readable summary of a DataDiff comparison

schema.title: Response Get Diff Summary V1 Api V1 Datadiffs  Datadiff Id  Summary Results Get
